title: Chamaesyce celastroides
intro: Chamaesyce celastroides, also known as the stinging spurge, is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help to alleviate pain, reduce fever, and support immune function. The bioactive constituents of Chamaesyce celastroides include diterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Chamaesyce celastroides is commonly prepared as a decoction, tincture, or ointment, and is used to treat a range of ailments, including skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory infections.
